titlestmt.titleproper: New York State College of Human Ecology Ghana Project records23-2-2703
titlestmt.author: Compiled by KJ Hillgren

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-08-20 18:19:38 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: New York State College of Human Ecology Ghana Project records

origination.label: creator
origination.corpname: New York State College of Human Ecology. Ghana Project

did.physdesc: Reports, correspondence, lists, syllabi, plans, clippings, photographs, and other materials.
did.abstract: Ghana Project records, including description of the project, reports, correspondence, lists, syllabi, plans, clippings, and photographs and the work done by Professor Kathleen Rhodes.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Ghana Project records, including description of the project, reports, correspondence, lists, syllabi, plans, clippings, and photographs and the work done by Professor Kathleen Rhodes.

p.ref: D. Extension Classes and Workshop Photos

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: New York State College of Human Ecology Ghana Project records, #23-2-2703. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

Series III. Documents
Description of Project; "The International Heritage of Home Economics in USA" by Kathleen Rhodes, AHEA-Ghana.
Progress reports on the Cornell-Ghana project

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes progress report 1 from 1964, report 2 from 1965 and report 3 from 1966.

did.unittitle: Closing words spoken at the reception of the National Education Association
Report by Kathleen Rhodes on visit to Ghana
Proposed projects in Ghana
Correspondence
Survey Conducted by Students at Winneba College and student autobiographies; April 1963 Report on 2nd Visit to Ghana by Kathleen Rhodes
Correspondence regarding Agreement with Ghana Steering committee minutes.
Project Statement (revised); Oct., Nov., Dec. correspondence.
Correspondence and reports from Ghana

Plans for and reports of consultant conference in Ghana; Reports from consultants, needs of Home Science program and proposed budget; Arrangements for visitors from Ghana. Report from Wood on Ghana situation. Correspondence regarding Home Demonstration Agents participation.

Proposed phases of Cornell-Ghana project; Correspondence regarding Rhodes visits to Ghana; Report by Flemming (University of Ghana); Arrangements for visit by Jiagge (Ghana); Report regarding Cornell-Ghana project Wilson.
Advisory Committee minutes; Regulations for Diploma in Home Science; Needs of Home Science Program (Rhodes); statement of project; Reports (Newkirk and Feldman)
Letters from Newkirk; clippings from Ghana Times; Advisory Committee minutes, personnel recruitment for '65-'66.
Summary report Sept.-Jan. (Newkirk); Letters to and from Newkirk regarding salary funding.
Letters from Narrowsburg Home Demonstration unit; Colson (U of Ghana) to Wilson (U of Ghana); Flemming (U of Ghana) to Colson.

Advisory Committee Minutes (3/3/65); Letters Canoyer (CU) to Brown (Ghana); Wood to Newkirk; Wilson to Canoyer; Wood to Flemming; Canoyer to Doulo (Ghana); Newkirk to Wood; Wood to Armbruster; O'Brien (U of Ghana) to Wood, Newkirk to Damatey (Ghana); Feldman and Newkirk to Wood; Canoyer to O'Brien.

Institutions Cooperating with CU-Ghana program. Advisory Committee minutes; letters Canoyer to Miller (Ghana); Canoyer to Pres. Perkins; Newkirk to Wood; Project report Feb.-March (Newkirk, memoranda on future projects, review of commitments)
Advisory Comm. memo; report to college faculty and minutes; correspondence need for Home Science degree; recommendations regarding Home Science (U of Ghana).
Letters Wood and Newkirk; Communications regarding project development.
Reports of social science consultant; memo of understanding (CU and U of Ghana); Letters Muller (CU) to Canoyer; Wood to Edzii (U of Ghana) and Newkirk; Newkirk to Wood; Advisory Committee minutes.
Letters, Canoyer to Middlemast (H Dagent); Muller to Wood; Wood to Olds; Ambassador Williams; Newkirk; Kwapong (U of Ghana); Anderegg; Review of memo of understanding and budget.
Memos to Advisory Committee regarding plans for HSC building at U of Ghana; award of Fulbright to V. Cukler for 66-67; letter Canoyer to Addo (Ghana)
Report (Newkirk); letter Newkirk regarding building plans; Advisory Committee minutes; Revised draft of Memo of Understanding.
Comments on Ghana contract and memo of understanding; Final report on Cornell-Ghana project.
Letter Wood to Cutter; memos from Cutter; draft of proposed site visit to U of Ghana by USAID.

Advisory Committee memo regarding planning HSC facilities at University of Ghana; description of program for FAO; letters between Wood and Cutter; Statements; "Education of Women in Ghana"; "Women's Education and Development of Community Health."
Winneba College syllabi; Nutrition; Applied Chemistry and Physics; Regulations for 1963; Plan for '65 grads.
Winneba College Regulations; statement of project.
Winneba College syllabi for Foods and Nutrition; Social Science; Regulations for Home Science Diploma
Materials on clothing and textiles for home science diploma.
Research Studies; Home Science course 1964-1965
Requests for financial assistance
Newspaper clippings: Ghana
Newspaper clippings: Ghana
Newspaper clippings: Ghana
Lati Hyde-Forster's closing speech in a letter from Helen G. Canoyer
Series IV. Memorabilia
Two silver trays and a pin - MU#1133
